plan: Create `src/modules/fingerprint-receipt.ts` with a `formatSnapshotToReceiptRows(snapshot: SignalSnapshot): ReceiptRow[]` function that transforms collected signals into human-readable receipt rows: timezone (e.g. 'America/New_York'), languages (comma-separated list), platform (as-is), touch support ('Supported' / 'Not supported'), CPU threads (e.g. '8 threads' or 'Unavailable'), Do Not Track ('Enabled' / 'Disabled' / 'Not set')
Handle missing/sentinel values gracefully — map MISSING.unknown and MISSING.unavailable to user-friendly display strings like 'Unavailable' rather than exposing raw sentinel values

Create `tests/us-012/fingerprint-receipt.test.ts` with tests covering: each acceptance criterion field is present and correctly formatted, missing value fallback formatting, integration test rendering snapshot data through createReceipt(), export contract verification

Wire up in `src/app/main.ts` or `src/layout/shell.ts`: call collectSnapshot(), pass through formatSnapshotToReceiptRows(), then render via createReceipt('Fingerprint Receipt', rows) into the .dashboard container
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all checks pass
testing_recommendations: Test each field formatter independently: timezone string passthrough, languages array joining, platform passthrough, touch boolean to human string, hardwareConcurrency number to '8 threads' format, doNotTrack to 'Enabled'/'Disabled'/'Not set'
Test missing value handling: pass snapshots with MISSING.unknown and MISSING.unavailable sentinels for each field and verify user-friendly fallback text
Test integration: formatSnapshotToReceiptRows returns exactly 6 ReceiptRow objects with correct labels
Test DOM rendering: pass formatted rows through createReceipt() and verify all 6 rows appear in the DOM with correct text content
Test that CPU threads row uses conditional 'when available' logic — shows value when hardwareConcurrency is a number, shows fallback when unavailable
risks: Minimal risk — this is a straightforward data formatting layer between two existing modules (US-009 snapshot and US-011 receipt component)
Browser API mocking in tests needs care: hardwareConcurrency and doNotTrack have inconsistent support across browsers, ensure sentinel fallbacks are tested
Human-readable formatting choices (e.g. 'Not set' vs 'Unknown' for Do Not Track) should match the project's content rules — no exaggeration, concise wording
